content.text:
<user_query>
Critique this current objective: user saw live Fuyao sweep dispatch succeed with status/verification JSON showing one UNKNOWN and others running/waiting_for_logs. Task: identify blocking issues, next actions, and whether extra verification is required. Use files: ~/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao_sweep_dispatcher.sh and ~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh and dispatch artifacts under ~/.cursor/tmp/deploy_fuyao_sweep_runs/seed-sweep-20260311-190936-7035. Focus on objective summary, risk severity, and concrete follow-up commands/logic. Provide concise recommendations.
</user_query>

<git_status>
This is the git status at the start of the conversation. Note that this status is a snapshot in time, and will not update during the conversation.

Git repo: /Users/HanHu/software/motion_rl

M humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_deploy.sh
 M humanoid-gym/scripts/fuyao_train.sh
?? hyperparameter-suggestion.md
</git_status>

<agent_transcripts>
Agent transcripts (past chats) live in /Users/HanHu/.cursor/projects/Users-HanHu-software-motion-rl/agent-transcripts. They have names like <uuid>.jsonl, cite them to the user as [<title for chat <=6 words>](<uuid excluding .jsonl>). NEVER cite subagent transcripts/IDs; you can only cite parent uuids. Don't discuss the folder structure.
</agent_transcripts>

<agent_skills>
When users ask you to perform tasks, check if any of the available skills below can help complete the task more effectively. Skills provide specialized capabilities and domain knowledge. To use a skill, read the skill file at the provided absolute path using the ReadFile tool, then follow the instructions within. When a skill is relevant, read and follow it IMMEDIATELY as your first action. NEVER just announce or mention a skill without actually reading and following it. Only use skills listed below.

<available_skills description="Skills the agent can use. Use the ReadFile tool with the provided absolute path to fetch full contents.">
<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/checkout-for-huh8/SKILL.md">Derive and checkout huh8-prefixed git branches while preserving already-prefixed branches, and prompt for explicit suffix when creating a new branch from a huh8 source branch. Use when the user asks to create, rename, or checkout branches with huh8 naming or company-ID branch conventions.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/commit-tag-prefix/SKILL.md">Prepends git commit messages with an angle-bracketed type tag and enforces a consistent format. Use when creating commit messages, preparing git commit text, or when the user asks to commit staged changes.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/hp-sweep-orchestrator/SKILL.md">Orchestrates hyperparameter sweep training jobs on Fuyao. Use when the user mentions hyperparameter sweep, parameter search, training sweep, sweep jobs, or wants to deploy multiple training runs with different config values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/motion-rl-isaacgym-exec/SKILL.md">Executes shell commands inside the isaacgym container for the Motion RL workspace. Use when the user mentions Motion RL, motion_rl, isaacgym-motion_rl workspace, or asks to run commands in that workspace.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/plotjuggler-udp-fuzzy/SKILL.md">Generates and updates PlotJuggler layout XML and streaming XML using UDP JSON signals. Use when the user mentions PlotJuggler, layout files, streaming config, UDP telemetry, fuzzy signal names, or wants fuzzy-to-exact signal mapping.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/policy-lineage-tracker/SKILL.md">Interactive conversational agent for policy lineage experiment tracking. Parses natural-language requests, gathers missing context through guided questions, and calls the tracker CLI to record nodes, export mermaid diagrams, and populate lineage tables. Use when the user mentions policy lineage, experiment tracking, mutations, runs, tasks, tracker nodes, exporting lineage graphs, or describes training results.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/reward-design-analyzer/SKILL.md">Analyzes RL reward design for a registered task by tracing config inheritance, categorizing all reward terms, ranking importance, identifying external disturbances, and producing a Markdown report with tuning suggestions. Use when the user asks to analyze rewards, understand reward design, trace reward configs, tune rewards, or wants a reward breakdown for a task in the motion_rl / humanoid-gym codebase.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sweep-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic dispatch and verification of Fuyao hyperparameter sweeps.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/sync-after-skill-create/SKILL.md">Legacy fallback for toolbox sync guidance. Prefer the personal rule `~/.cursor/rules/sync-toolbox-after-toolbox-edits.mdc` for primary behavior when editing toolbox assets.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-rule/SKILL.md">Create Cursor rules for persistent AI guidance. Use when you want to create a rule, add coding standards, set up project conventions, configure file-specific patterns, create RULE.md files, or asks about .cursor/rules/ or AGENTS.md.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/create-skill/SKILL.md">Guides users through creating effective Agent Skills for Cursor. Use when you want to create, write, or author a new skill, or asks about skill structure, best practices, or SKILL.md format.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills-cursor/update-cursor-settings/SKILL.md">Modify Cursor/VSCode user settings in settings.json. Use when you want to change editor settings, preferences, configuration, themes, font size, tab size, format on save, auto save, keybindings, or any settings.json values.</agent_skill>

<agent_skill fullPath="/Users/HanHu/.cursor/skills/deploy-fuyao/SKILL.md">Deterministic Fuyao deployment flow with strict validation, branch sync, and confirmation.</agent_skill>
</available_skills>
</agent_skills>

**Objective summary**
- Dispatch is not the root failure: all five combos were created with `status: "success"` and `reason: "submitted"` in `status/combo_XXXX.json`, and each dispatch log contains a successful submit line.
- The verification layer is exposing startup/telemetry ambiguity, not a confirmed training failure for 4/5 jobs.
- `combo_0001` is the exception: `job_status: "unknown"` and `training_status: "unknown"` in `verification/combo_0001.json` is the only truly blocking signal.

**Critical findings (severity ordered)**

1. **High — `combo_0001` remains `unknown/unknown` at verification time**
- Evidence path: `verification/combo_0001.json` + `check_job_status` in `~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh` only sets known states via parsing `fuyao info -n <job>` output.
- Why this blocks: the script cannot derive status from `fuyao info` for this job, so the system cannot distinguish between transient API/auth failures, a scheduling visibility gap, or a job that failed before reportable output.
- Result: cannot treat the sweep as healthy yet.

2. **High — Four jobs are in `running + waiting_for_logs` with `UNKNOWN` verdict**
- Evidence path: `verification/combo_0002..0005.json`, `check_training_started` markers in `~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh`.
- Why it matters: this is not an error state, but it is not a readiness signal either. Training may still be initializing, stuck before emit points, or producing logs under different text than expected.
- Key behavior in script: training classification is limited to explicit marker strings (`num_learning_iterations`, `Mean reward`, `Starting training`, etc.) and falls back to `waiting_for_logs` when absent.

3. **Medium — Repeated remote dispatch warning (`fatal: HEAD does not point to a branch` + upstream warning)**
- Evidence path: all `logs/combo_XXXX.dispatch.log`.
- Why it matters: dispatch succeeded anyway, but this indicates non-ideal git state in remote execution context and can affect reproducibility/troubleshooting confidence in edge cases.

4. **Medium — Python compatibility warning from Fuyao console**
- Evidence path: all dispatch logs show `⚠️ The new version of fuyao console will only support Python 3.10 ~ 3.12`.
- Why it matters: warning-only today, but it increases future fragility and should be treated as technical debt before long-running campaigns.

5. **Low — `verdict` semantics are “optimistic-ambiguous”**
- A non-terminal, non-failing state (`running + waiting_for_logs`) is intentionally surfaced as `UNKNOWN`.
- Why it matters: this is good for safety, but it requires explicit follow-up polling before declaring pass/fail.

**Concrete next actions (recommended order)**

1. **Deep-dive the blocked job first (`combo_0001`)**
```bash
RUN_ROOT=~/.cursor/scripts/deploy_fuyao_sweep_runs/seed-sweep-20260311-190936-7035
JOB_NAME=bifrost-2026031210095300-huh8

ssh remote.kernel.fuyo "fuyao info -n '${JOB_NAME}'"
ssh remote.kernel.fuyo "fuyao log --job-name='${JOB_NAME}' --rank=0 --show-stdout | sed -n '1,260p'"
```
- If `fuyao info` returns weird/empty output repeatedly, capture raw command output with a wrapper and compare with parser expectations in `verify_fuyao_jobs.sh`.

2. **Re-run verification with longer patience, no immediate failover**
```bash
bash ~/.cursor/scripts/verify_fuyao_jobs.sh \
  --run-root "$RUN_ROOT" \
  --poll-interval 60 \
  --max-attempts 40
```
- This is the right move for the four `running + waiting_for_logs` jobs; if they remain stuck, the issue is likely pre-training initialization.

3. **Cross-check each uncertain job against logs directly (not just parser output)**
```bash
for job in bifrost-2026031210095300-huh8 bifrost-...; do
  ssh remote.kernel.fuyao "fuyao log --job-name='$job' --rank=0 --show-stdout | tail -n 200"
done
```
- Replace `...` with the remaining four job names from `verification/*.json`.
- Look for startup blocker messages (`ImportError`, `CUDA`, filesystem paths, git/checkout failures, missing files).

4. **If `combo_0001` is irreparably unknown, resubmit that combo only**
- Use the same resolved command/env as the sweep dispatcher produced, then re-verify only that job.
- Keep the original artifacts; this makes deltas attributable.

5. **Prevent recurrence**
- Set upstream before dispatching:
```bash
git checkout huh/dev_r01_v12_sa_wholestate_female-balance
git push -u origin huh/dev_r01_v12_sa_wholestate_female-balance
```
- Coordinate with platform owner to move remote Python to 3.10–3.12 for Fuyao CLI compatibility.

**Extra verification required?**
Yes.  
Current evidence is sufficient to continue observing but **not** sufficient to declare the sweep successful. The minimum additional verification threshold is:
- one more poll pass with longer window,
- raw `fuyao info` and `fuyao log` checks for `combo_0001`,
- explicit confirmation of first training markers for at least one of the waiting jobs.

If you want, I can provide a strict “go/no-go” checklist (one-page) for deciding when to continue, resubmit only failed combos, or abort and restart.
